page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8i.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores with a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. Because the threads per core ratio is 1.0, each of the 16 vCPUs maps directly to a dedicated physical core without hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance operates on a KVM hypervisor and does not include any bundled local storage, meaning all storage must be provisioned externally. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

With a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance represents a standard general-purpose resource profile. The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent compute performance without resource contention from hyperthreading, which is a key tradeoff compared to shared-core or hyperthreaded instances. It is suitable for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance and balanced memory, such as application servers, web serving, containerized microservices, and medium-sized databases that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8i.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via a KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8475B processor operating at 2.7 GHz, providing 16 dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The system features 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage, requiring external storage volumes, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Lacking hyperthreading, it delivers highly predictable compute performance. This resource profile is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, application hosting, and containerized deployments.
